Such trilateration is liable to rapid accumulation of azimuth error.
And there is also an azimuth thruster that can be swung down from the forward part of the hull if an extra push is required.
These are similar to the azimuth thrusters already used on some vessels: propellers on pods that can be rotated to push the ship in different directions, making a rudder unnecessary.
